key_trials: MF4414, MF4434, Kristensen et al., Paterson et al., Tubiana-Hulin et al.
pico_population: Patients with bone metastases from breast cancer; adults eligible for bisphosphonate therapy

indication: Bone metastases from breast cancer. Oral tablet formulation for long-term maintenance therapy at home.
icer_note: Cost-minimisation analysis; no ICER calculated
key_trials: MF4414, MF4434, MF4265, Body et al. 2007
pico_population: Patients with bone metastases from breast cancer requiring long-term bisphosphonate therapy

indication: Extension of listing for treatment of four rare diseases: dermatofibrosarcoma protuberans (DFSP), hypereosinophilic syndrome/chronic eosinophilic leukaemia (HES/CEL), myelodysplastic/myeloproliferative diseases (MDS/MPD), and aggressive systemic mastocytosis (ASM).
comparator: Standard medical management, radiotherapy (DFSP), chemotherapy (HES/CEL, MDS/MPD, ASM)
icer_note: ICER not stated in extracted text
pico_population: Adult patients with unresectable, recurrent and/or metastatic DFSP; HES/CEL with FIP1L1-PDGFRA fusion gene; MDS/MPD with PDGFR gene re-arrangements; ASM with FIP1L1-PDGFRA fusion gene and absence of D816V c-kit mutation, where conventional therapies have failed.

indication: Management and maintenance of hyperphosphataemia in patients with chronic kidney disease on dialysis whose serum phosphate is not controlled on other products.
icer_note: ICER not stated in available document excerpt
pico_population: Adults with chronic kidney disease on dialysis whose serum phosphate is not controlled on other products, with serum phosphate greater than 1.6 mmol per L or serum calcium times phosphate product greater than 4.0

indication: Treatment of patients with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma in combination with dexamethasone, for patients who have undergone at least one prior therapy.
key_trials: Weber 2007 (Study 009), Dimopoulos 2007 (Study 010), APEX trial, Palumbo 2004, Offidani 2007
pico_population: Adults with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma (defined as progressive disease after prior therapy or intolerance/contraindication to treatment) who have received at least one prior therapy

indication: Relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma in patients who have failed thalidomide therapy or have severe intolerance/toxicity to thalidomide, in combination with dexamethasone.
comparator: bortezomib (monotherapy or in combination with dexamethasone)
icer_note: ICER values redacted or not stated in provided excerpt
pico_population: Adult patients with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma who have received at least 1 prior therapy (other than thalidomide), have undergone or are ineligible for primary stem cell transplant, and have experienced treatment failure after at least 4 weeks of thalidomide at ≥100 mg daily.

indication: Hypertension in patients who are not adequately controlled with either lercanidipine or enalapril monotherapy.
icer_note: Cost-minimisation analysis; no ICER calculated
pico_population: Patients with mild to moderate essential hypertension not adequately controlled with either lercanidipine or enalapril monotherapy

indication: Treatment of primary generalised tonic clonic seizures (PGTCS) and generalised myoclonic seizures (juvenile myoclonic epilepsy) as add-on therapy in patients with idiopathic generalised epilepsy.
icer_note: Cost-minimisation basis; no ICER calculated
pico_population: Adults and children from 4 years of age with idiopathic generalised epilepsy where the predominant seizure type is primary generalised tonic clonic or myoclonic (juvenile myoclonic epilepsy), inadequately controlled by other anti-epileptic drugs.
primary_endpoint: Median percentage reduction in seizure rate; responder rates (50% and 100% seizure reduction)

indication: Treatment of advanced idiopathic Parkinson's disease with severe disabling motor fluctuations not adequately controlled by oral therapy.
comparator: standard medical management (suboptimal oral medication and continuous subcutaneous apomorphine infusion)
rejection_reasons: product did not meet all criteria for listing under the Highly Specialised Drug Program
pico_population: Patients with advanced idiopathic Parkinson's disease with severe motor fluctuations despite individually optimised conventional treatment (oral levodopa, apomorphine, amantadine, dopamine agonists)

indication: Treatment of antiretroviral-experienced adult patients infected with CCR5-tropic HIV-1 in combination with other antiretrovirals. Indicated for patients who have failed or have resistance to 3 or more different antiretroviral regimens.
pico_population: Antiretroviral-experienced adult patients infected with CCR5-tropic HIV-1 who have failed previous treatment with or have resistance to 3 different antiretroviral regimens (including at least 1 NNRTI, at least 1 NRTI, and at least 2 protease inhibitors), with viral load greater than 5000 copies/mL

indication: Treatment of moderately severe Alzheimer's disease in patients with baseline (Standardised) Mini-Mental State Examination score of 10-14.
icer_note: Cost-minimisation comparison basis; not presented as ICER range
pico_population: Adults with moderately severe Alzheimer's disease with baseline (S)MMSE score of 10-14

indication: Treatment of superficial basal cell carcinoma (sBCC) in patients who cannot have surgical excision, cryotherapy, or curettage with diathermy. The lesion must be previously untreated and diagnosis confirmed by biopsy.
key_trials: T307, T308, Soler et al. 2001, Surrenti et al. 2007, Sterry et al. 2002, Marks et al. 2001, Peris et al. 2005, Vidal et al. 2004, Geisse et al. 2002
pico_population: Adult patients (18 years or older) with biopsy-confirmed primary superficial basal cell carcinoma where surgical excision, cryotherapy, or curettage with diathermy are inappropriate

indication: Type 1 Gaucher disease in patients who cannot be or have not been treated successfully with enzyme replacement therapy (ERT), or for whom ERT is not a therapeutic option.
icer_note: No ICER presented; cost-utility analysis not performed
pico_population: Adult patients with mild to moderate Type 1 Gaucher disease who cannot be treated with ERT due to contraindications (poor venous access, severe needle phobia, hypersensitivity reactions) or inability to tolerate ERT

indication: First-line treatment of narcolepsy in patients with excessive daytime sleepiness and either a definite history of cataplexy, mean sleep latency ≤10 minutes on Multiple Sleep Latency Test, or pathologically rapid REM development on EEG.
comparator: placebo (dexamphetamine no longer considered appropriate comparator due to safety concerns)
icer_note: No economic analysis presented in submission; PBAC noted this omission was inappropriate
rejection_reasons: uncertain long-term clinical benefit, lack of economic analysis, unclear clinical importance of surrogate outcomes, uncertain relative net clinical effect particularly long-term, unclear patient-relevant benefit (e.g. ability to drive or return to work)
pico_population: adults with narcolepsy meeting diagnostic criteria including excessive daytime sleepiness for at least 3 months and either definite cataplexy history, mean sleep latency ≤10 minutes on MSLT, or pathological REM on EEG, with absence of other medical/psychiatric disorders explaining hypersomnia

indication: Treatment of chronic phase and accelerated phase Philadelphia chromosome positive chronic myeloid leukaemia (CML) in adult patients intolerant of or resistant to at least one prior therapy including imatinib.
pico_population: Adult patients with chronic phase or accelerated phase Philadelphia chromosome positive chronic myeloid leukaemia who have failed or are intolerant of imatinib or other second-line therapy (dasatinib).

indication: Treatment of infections due to influenza A and B viruses in adults and children aged 1 year and older. Treatment should commence as soon as possible, but no later than 48 hours after onset of initial symptoms.
icer_note: Document truncated; economic analysis section not fully provided
rejection_reasons: Uncertain efficacy (mixed and inconsistent secondary outcomes, no firm evidence of reduced hospitalisation or secondary illness incidence), marginal symptom reduction (16.8 hours average in ITT population with limited statistical significance in non-healthy adults and children), significant adverse events (nausea, vomiting, diarrhoea with substantial prevalence expected in patient population)
pico_population: Adults and children aged 1 year and older with influenza-like illness (intention-to-treat population); stratified by risk: otherwise healthy adults, at-risk adults and elderly, and children

indication: Treatment of symptoms of urge urinary incontinence and urgency in patients with overactive bladder.
rejection_reasons: non-inferiority not demonstrated in Trial 017, response may be over-inflated due to use of non-TGA-approved doses in efficacy outcomes, uncertainty regarding comparative efficacy claim
pico_population: Adults with overactive bladder presenting with urge urinary incontinence and urgency symptoms, including patients with mixed incontinence.

indication: Urge urinary incontinence or urgency due to detrusor instability in patients who cannot tolerate oral oxybutynin or propantheline, as second-line therapy.
icer_note: ICER estimated at < $15,000 per QALY, but wide confidence intervals ranged from dominance to placebo being dominant.
rejection_reasons: Key trials did not recruit patients intolerant to oral oxybutynin as required by the restriction; Trial 009 recruited mostly previously untreated patients; Trial 011 recruited patients tolerant to anticholinergic therapy; trial participants had more severe disease than the target population; uncertain application to the requested patient population; concerns regarding clinical effectiveness and cost-effectiveness in the target population.
pico_population: Patients with urge or mixed urinary incontinence due to detrusor instability who cannot tolerate oral oxybutynin or propantheline.

indication: Advanced breast cancer after failure of prior therapy which includes an anthracycline. Nanoparticle albumin-bound (nab) paclitaxel formulation for metastatic breast cancer.
icer_note: ICER not stated in document excerpt
pico_population: Patients with metastatic breast cancer after failure of prior anthracycline therapy, including both first-line and later-line treatment settings

indication: Treatment of K-RAS wild-type metastatic colorectal cancer after failure of treatment with fluoropyrimidine, irinotecan and oxaliplatin.
pico_population: Adults with K-RAS wild-type, EGFR-expressing metastatic colorectal cancer with ECOG performance status ≤2 who have failed fluoropyrimidine, irinotecan and oxaliplatin-based chemotherapy.

indication: Treatment of patients with end-stage chronic renal disease receiving dialysis who have secondary hyperparathyroidism (iPTH > 300 pg/mL with elevated phosphate or calcium levels).
key_trials: Teng 2003, Tentori 2006b, Dobrez 2004, Kalantar-Zadeh 2006, Lee 2007, Young 2006
rejection_reasons: insufficient evidence of superiority over comparator, non-randomised observational studies prone to selection bias, no head-to-head trials comparing oral paricalcitol vs oral calcitriol, uncertain relative effectiveness of oral formulations, data primarily on IV paricalcitol while oral formulation requested
pico_population: Adult patients with end-stage chronic renal disease on dialysis with secondary hyperparathyroidism (iPTH >300 pg/mL and elevated phosphate or calcium)

indication: Primary prophylaxis for chemotherapy-induced neutropenia (CIN) in patients with breast cancer treated with docetaxel, including both early breast cancer (HER2-positive with docetaxel plus trastuzumab) and advanced breast cancer (docetaxel monotherapy).
comparator: Secondary prophylaxis with pegfilgrastim (early breast cancer); no G-CSF (advanced breast cancer)
icer_note: Cost-effectiveness analysis conducted but ICER values not provided in the available text excerpt
pico_population: Patients with breast cancer (both metastatic/advanced and non-metastatic/early, HER2-positive with trastuzumab) receiving docetaxel chemotherapy

indication: Primary prophylaxis of chemotherapy-induced febrile neutropenia in chronic lymphocytic leukaemia (CLL) patients treated with fludarabine and cyclophosphamide (FC).
icer_note: No economic evaluation presented; cost-minimisation basis assumed relative to filgrastim.
pico_population: Patients with chronic lymphocytic leukaemia undergoing induction and consolidation therapy with fludarabine and cyclophosphamide with high risk of febrile neutropenia.

indication: Treatment of facial lipoatrophy caused by antiretroviral therapy in HIV positive patients.
pico_population: HIV positive patients with facial lipoatrophy caused by antiretroviral therapy

indication: Prophylaxis of invasive fungal infections (IFIs) among patients 13 years of age or older who are at high risk of developing these infections, including those with prolonged neutropenia or haematopoietic stem cell transplant (HSCT) recipients.
icer_note: ICER not stated in extracted text
pico_population: Patients 13 years of age or older at high risk of invasive fungal infections, including those with anticipated neutropenia, haematopoietic stem cell transplant recipients, graft versus host disease patients, high-risk lung transplant recipients, and those receiving high-dose corticosteroids.

indication: Treatment of severe idiopathic (primary) Restless Legs Syndrome in patients meeting all four diagnostic criteria with baseline IRLSRS score ≥21 points.
icer_note: Cost-minimisation analysis used; no ICER range applicable
pico_population: Adults with severe idiopathic Restless Legs Syndrome meeting all four diagnostic criteria with baseline IRLSRS score ≥21 points

indication: Treatment of HIV-1 infection in combination with other antiretroviral agents in antiretroviral-experienced patients with evidence of viral replication and/or CD4 cell counts <500 cells/mm³, who have failed or are resistant to 3 different antiretroviral regimens including at least one NNRTI, one NRTI, and one protease inhibitor.
icer_note: ICER not stated in available PSD excerpt
pico_population: Treatment-experienced HIV-1 infected adults with evidence of viral replication (HIV RNA >10,000 copies/mL) and/or CD4 cell counts <500 cells/mm³, resistant to multiple antiretroviral classes

indication: Treatment of idiopathic Parkinson's disease from early stage to advanced disease, as monotherapy or in combination with levodopa.
pico_population: Adults with idiopathic Parkinson's disease from early stage to advanced disease, suitable for treatment with a non-ergot dopamine agonist as monotherapy or in combination with levodopa.
primary_endpoint: UPDRS ADL and motor subscales (early PD); absolute time 'off' in hours (advanced PD)

indication: Management of type 2 diabetes with obesity (BMI ≥30 kg/m²) and abnormalities in HDL and triglycerides in adults aged 18–65 years who have not adequately responded to diet and/or exercise alone.
comparator: lifestyle modification (reduced calorie diet and/or exercise)
icer_note: Cost-effectiveness estimates were revised but uncertainty about cost-effectiveness remained a reason for rejection.
rejection_reasons: doubts about extent and duration of clinical benefit, uncertain cost-effectiveness, low proportion of patients achieving substantial weight loss (>10%), post hoc subgroup analyses, uncommon individual responses representing substantial weight loss
pico_population: Adults aged 18–65 years with type 2 diabetes and obesity (BMI ≥30 kg/m²) who are normotensive or have adequately controlled hypertension (<145/90 mmHg), have not adequately responded to diet/exercise alone, and have either triglycerides >150 mg/dL or low HDL cholesterol.
primary_endpoint: Weight loss (≥5% and ≥10% of body weight)

indication: Type 2 diabetes mellitus in combination with metformin or sulfonylurea when a combination of metformin and sulfonylurea is contraindicated or not tolerated, in patients with HbA1c >7% despite monotherapy.
key_trials: P801, P020, P024, P035, Gomez-Perez 2002, Fonseca 2000, Einhorn 2000, Matthews 2005, Umpierrez 2006, Phillips 2001, Hanefeld 2007, Lebovitz 2001
pico_population: Adults aged 18 years and older with type 2 diabetes mellitus who have failed dietary measures and exercise, with HbA1c >7% despite treatment with metformin or sulfonylurea monotherapy, where dual combination with sulfonylurea and metformin is contraindicated or not tolerated.

indication: Improvement of body composition and treatment of short stature associated with Prader-Willi Syndrome in paediatric patients
key_trials: Carrel 1999, Festen 2007a, Festen 2007b, Haqq 2003, Hauffa 1997, Lindgren 1997, Whitman 2004, Angulo 2007
pico_population: paediatric patients with Prader-Willi Syndrome and growth hormone deficiency

indication: Initial and continuing treatment of advanced (unresectable or metastatic) renal cell carcinoma in patients with WHO performance status 2 or less, where the patient is not experiencing or is free of disease progression.
icer_note: ICER values redacted or not fully stated in provided text; document truncated
pico_population: Patients with advanced (unresectable or metastatic) renal cell carcinoma, WHO performance status 2 or less; Trial 11213 enrolled previously treated patients with prior systemic therapy failure (second-line setting); Trial 11848 enrolled previously untreated patients (first-line setting)

indication: Treatment of gastrointestinal stromal tumour (GIST) after failure of imatinib mesylate due to resistance or intolerance.
icer_note: ICER range per QALY gained $105,000–$200,000 (base case after Pre-Sub-Committee corrections); per LYG $75,000–$105,000. Range reflects sensitivity to key parameters.
pico_population: Adults with metastatic or unresectable gastrointestinal stromal tumour (GIST) who have failed imatinib mesylate treatment due to resistance or intolerance.

indication: Treatment of advanced (unresectable or metastatic) renal cell carcinoma (RCC) in patients with Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) performance status of 0 or 1.
comparator: best supportive care (BSC) without hormonal support/placebo
key_trials: Trial A618-1034, MRC Renal Cancer Trial
pico_population: Adults with advanced (unresectable or metastatic) renal cell carcinoma, clear cell disease, ECOG performance status 0 or 1, receiving first-line therapy

indication: Extension of listing to include prophylaxis and treatment of lung allograft rejection in lung transplant recipients, for both maintenance therapy and management of acute rejection.
icer_note: ICER not stated in this PSD — economic analysis appears limited; cost-effectiveness claim noted in background but specific figures redacted or not presented
pico_population: Adults and children receiving lung allografts, requiring maintenance immunosuppression or management of acute or chronic rejection (including prophylaxis of bronchiolitis obliterans syndrome)
primary_endpoint: Acute rejection (freedom from acute rejection ≥A2), Bronchiolitis Obliterans Syndrome

indication: Relief of lower urinary tract symptoms (LUTS) associated with benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH)
pico_population: Men older than 40 years with BPH and at least moderate lower urinary tract symptoms (LUTS)

indication: Lower urinary tract symptoms due to benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH), including hesitancy, dribbling, nocturia, frequency and urgency.
icer_note: Range of 45,000–75,000 AUD per QALY (12-week trial duration) and 15,000–45,000 AUD per QALY (12-month extrapolation); PBAC considered these unacceptably high and uncertain.
rejection_reasons: Unacceptably high and uncertain incremental cost-utility ratio, uncertain translation into utilities (mean incremental QALY gain of 0.0113 per patient per year), small clinical benefit with uncertain clinical significance, substantial existing private market
pico_population: Adults with lower urinary tract symptoms due to benign prostatic hyperplasia

indication: Treatment of chronic hepatitis B in nucleoside analogue naïve patients aged 16 years or older with evidence of viral replication and active liver inflammation.
icer_note: Economic analysis presented but ICER values not stated in extracted document text
key_trials: NV-02B-007, NV-02B-015, Chang et al 2006, Lai et al 2006
pico_population: Patients aged 16 years or older with chronic hepatitis B (HBeAg-positive and HBeAg-negative) who are nucleoside analogue naïve, have compensated liver disease, evidence of viral replication, active liver inflammation, and histological evidence of chronic hepatitis

indication: Extension of listing to include treatment of chronic hepatitis B (CHB) in treatment-naïve patients and those who have failed antihepadnaviral therapy, in addition to existing HIV indication.
comparator: entecavir monohydrate 0.5 mg (nucleos(t)ide naïve patients); adefovir dipivoxil 10 mg (nucleos(t)ide experienced patients)
pico_population: Treatment-naïve chronic hepatitis B patients with histological evidence of chronic hepatitis and abnormal ALT or elevated HBV DNA; treatment-experienced patients who have failed antihepadnaviral therapy with repeatedly elevated ALT or persistently elevated HBV DNA despite prior therapy

indication: Treatment of glucocorticoid-induced osteoporosis (GIOP) and severe established osteoporosis in patients at high risk of fracture where other agents are unsuitable or have failed.
icer_note: ICER not stated in available text
pico_population: Postmenopausal women and men with severe established osteoporosis or glucocorticoid-induced osteoporosis at very high risk of fracture, including those with demonstrated new fractures despite 12 months of continuous anti-resorptive therapy or with contraindication/intolerance to anti-resorptive agents.

indication: Treatment of HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer in combination with chemotherapy (taxanes) for first-line treatment and as monotherapy for patients who have received prior chemotherapy.
comparator: taxanes (paclitaxel or docetaxel), aromatase inhibitors, vinorelbine, placebo
key_trials: Slamon 2001, Marty 2005, Gasparini 2006, TanDEM (B016216), Burstein 2007, Robert 2006
pico_population: Adults with HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer; first-line treatment in combination with taxanes or as monotherapy in previously treated patients

indication: Monotherapy for up to six months of an episode of acute mania or mixed episodes associated with bipolar I disorder.
icer_note: Cost-minimisation analysis; no ICER calculated
pico_population: Patients with acute mania or mixed episodes associated with bipolar I disorder

indication: Primary prevention of fractures in high-risk women aged over 70 years with BMD T-score < -3.0, and secondary prevention of fractures in post-menopausal women with minimal trauma fractures.
icer_note: Cost-utility analysis presented but ICER values not stated in extracted text; cost-minimisation basis used for secondary prevention
key_trials: Trial 2301, FIT-CFA, HIP, Heaney et al meta-analysis
pico_population: Women aged 70 years or older with BMD T-score ≤ -3.0 (primary prevention), or post-menopausal women with established osteoporosis and minimal trauma fracture (secondary prevention)
primary_endpoint: Fracture reduction (morphometric vertebral, non-vertebral, and hip fractures)

indication: Prevention of herpes zoster (shingles), prevention of postherpetic neuralgia (PHN), and reduction of acute and chronic zoster-associated pain in immunocompetent persons aged 60 years and over.
icer_note: ICER ranges from $15,000-$45,000 for ages 60-79 years, and $45,000-$75,000 for ages 80-100; not recommended for ages 80+ due to unacceptably high and uncertain cost-effectiveness ratio
rejection_reasons: For ages 80+: unacceptably high and uncertain cost-effectiveness ratio, lack of safety and efficacy data in persons over 80 years, clinical uncertainty regarding vaccine effectiveness differences between formulations, efficacy in those over 80, and concomitant pneumococcal vaccine administration
pico_population: Immunocompetent persons aged 60 years and over; ongoing cohort of 60-year-old individuals and catch-up cohort for individuals aged 61-79 years

indication: Treatment of moderate to severe spasticity of the upper limbs of children (2 years of age or older) with cerebral palsy.
icer_note: Cost-minimisation analysis performed; ICER values not explicitly stated in extracted text
key_trials: Lowe 2006, Boyd 2006, Wallen 2007, Speth 2005, Fehlings 2000, Russo 2007
pico_population: Children aged 2 years or older with cerebral palsy and moderate to severe upper limb spasticity (Ashworth score ≥2)
primary_endpoint: Goal Attainment Scaling (GAS) or Quality of Upper Extremity Skills Test (QUEST)

indication: Treatment of adults with paroxysmal nocturnal haemoglobinuria (PNH) who have inadequate clinical response or are intolerant to C5 inhibitor treatment.
icer_note: Cost-minimisation analysis performed; economic model from March 2022 deemed unreliable and replaced with CMA approach
risk_sharing_note: Proposed inclusion in existing C5 inhibitor risk sharing arrangement for eculizumab and ravulizumab; % rebate of initial pegcetacoplan script proposed to mitigate financial impact during concomitant administration phase
pico_population: Adults with paroxysmal nocturnal haemoglobinuria (PNH) who have inadequate clinical response or are intolerant to C5 inhibitor treatment

indication: Treatment of adults with paroxysmal nocturnal haemoglobinuria (PNH) who have inadequate clinical response to C5 inhibitor treatment (haemoglobin level <10.5 g/dL after ≥3 months of stable treatment).
icer_note: ICER information not stated in the provided PSD text
pico_population: Adults aged 18 years or older with paroxysmal nocturnal haemoglobinuria (PNH) who have inadequate clinical response to C5 inhibitor treatment (haemoglobin level <10.5 g/dL after ≥3 months of stable treatment)

indication: Non-subfoveal geographic atrophy secondary to age-related macular degeneration, with intact fovea and central vision threatened by GA lesion growth.
icer_note: Commercially sensitive pricing information redacted in PSD
pico_population: Adults with bilateral geographic atrophy secondary to AMD, with intact fovea (non-subfoveal, ≥1 µm from foveal centre), central vision threatened by GA lesion growth (≤750 µm from foveal centre or progressing toward it within macula), and fellow eye with subfoveal GA.
primary_endpoint: Change in total area of GA lesions at 12 months measured by fundus autofluorescence

indication: Primary prophylaxis for febrile neutropenia in patients with early stage breast cancer being treated with docetaxel and cyclophosphamide
icer_note: Cost-minimisation analysis presented; economic analysis incomplete in provided text
pico_population: Adult patients with early stage breast cancer receiving adjuvant chemotherapy with docetaxel and cyclophosphamide with the intention of achieving cure or substantial remission

indication: Chemotherapy-induced neutropenia in patients with specified malignancies (acute myeloid leukaemia, breast cancer, B-cell chronic lymphocytic leukaemia, Hodgkin disease, myeloma, squamous cell carcinoma of oral cavity/larynx/oropharynx/hypopharynx, germ cell tumours, non-Hodgkin lymphoma, sarcoma, central nervous system tumours, neuroblastoma) who meet specified clinical criteria.
icer_note: Not applicable — biosimilar submission; cost-minimisation approach implied
pico_population: Patients with chemotherapy-induced neutropenia across specified malignancies (acute myeloid leukaemia, breast cancer, B-cell chronic lymphocytic leukaemia, Hodgkin disease, myeloma, squamous cell carcinoma, germ cell tumours, non-Hodgkin lymphoma, sarcoma, central nervous system tumours, neuroblastoma) meeting clinical criteria

indication: Prophylaxis of febrile neutropenia in cancer patients receiving chemotherapy with curative intent, or secondary prophylaxis in patients with prior febrile neutropenia episodes.
comparator: no treatment (placebo); filgrastim and lipegfilgrastim noted as potential alternatives
icer_note: ICER expressed per febrile neutropenia event avoided; price-sensitive data redacted in document
pico_population: Cancer patients receiving chemotherapy with curative intent and ≥20% febrile neutropenia risk (primary prophylaxis), or patients with prior febrile neutropenia or prolonged severe neutropenia episodes requiring continued therapy (secondary prophylaxis)

indication: Chemotherapy-induced neutropenia in cancer patients receiving chemotherapy with curative or substantial remission intent, with either greater than 20% risk of febrile neutropenia, prolonged severe neutropenia for ≥7 days, or prior episodes of febrile neutropenia.
icer_note: No economic evaluation undertaken; minor submission for biosimilar listing
pico_population: Cancer patients receiving chemotherapy with intent of cure or substantial remission, at >20% risk of febrile neutropenia or prolonged severe neutropenia (≥7 days), or with prior episodes of febrile/prolonged severe neutropenia

indication: Chemotherapy-induced neutropenia in cancer patients receiving chemotherapy with intention of achieving cure or substantial remission, and at greater than 20% risk of developing febrile neutropenia or prolonged severe neutropenia.
icer_note: Not applicable — cost-minimisation comparison (biosimilar)
pico_population: Adult cancer patients receiving chemotherapy (specifically breast cancer patients on TAC chemotherapy) with intention of achieving cure or substantial remission, at greater than 20% risk of febrile neutropenia or prolonged severe neutropenia

indication: First-line treatment of patients with remitting-relapsing multiple sclerosis who are ambulatory and have experienced at least 2 documented attacks of neurological dysfunction in the preceding 2 years.
icer_note: Cost-minimisation analysis conducted; ICER not applicable
pico_population: Adults with clinically definite relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis confirmed by MRI, who are ambulatory without assistance and have experienced at least 2 documented neurological attacks in the preceding 2 years

indication: Chronic hepatitis B and C infection; potential use in myeloproliferative neoplasms (MPN) including essential thrombocythemia and polycythemia vera.
comparator: hydroxyurea (for MPN); interferon alfa-2a (for chronic myeloid leukaemia)
icer_note: No ICER calculated; minor submission for change in restriction level only
risk_sharing_note: Proposed subsidisation cap limiting financial risk to Commonwealth for use in conditions beyond MPN and chronic hepatitis B and C
key_trials: Phase III study in chronic myeloid leukaemia comparing peginterferon alfa-2a to interferon alfa-2a; Phase II single-arm studies in MPN; Studies comparing peginterferon alfa-2a to hydroxyurea in essential thrombocythemia and polycythemia vera
pico_population: Patients with chronic hepatitis B and C; patients with myeloproliferative neoplasms (MPN) including essential thrombocythemia and polycythemia vera unable to be treated with hydroxyurea

indication: Long-term enzyme replacement therapy in adult patients with confirmed Fabry disease and end-organ damage.
icer_note: Cost-minimisation approach versus migalastat; price premium redacted as '%' in submission
pico_population: Adults with confirmed Fabry disease; PBS population restricted to patients with end-organ damage (eGFR ≥30 mL/min/1.73m²)

indication: Hyperphenylalaninemia (HPA) due to phenylketonuria (PKU) in patients aged 16 years and over who are not responsive to sapropterin, with baseline blood phenylalanine levels ≥600 µmol/L.
icer_note: ICER values not stated in the extracted text
pico_population: Adults aged ≥16 years with HPA due to PKU who have baseline blood phenylalanine levels ≥600 µmol/L and have failed to achieve ≥30% reduction in blood phenylalanine with sapropterin trial

indication: Second-line treatment of acromegaly in patients who have not achieved biochemical control with maximum indicated doses of octreotide LAR or lanreotide ATG.
icer_note: Cost-minimisation analysis; ICER not calculated
pico_population: Adult patients with acromegaly who have failed to achieve biochemical control on maximum indicated doses of octreotide LAR (30 mg) or lanreotide ATG (120 mg) every 28 days for 24 weeks, with IGF-1 concentration greater than 1.3 times the upper limit of normal.

indication: Treatment of active acromegaly in patients who have failed to achieve biochemical control with somatostatin analogues (SSAs).
icer_note: Cost-utility analysis presented initially; pre-PBAC response changed to cost-minimisation analysis excluding cost offsets for diabetes. Specific ICER values redacted in document.
pico_population: Adults (≥18 years) with active acromegaly who have failed to achieve biochemical control with somatostatin analogues (octreotide LAR, lanreotide autogel, or pasireotide), after surgery and/or radiotherapy where appropriate/tolerated.

indication: Treatment of unresectable Stage III or Stage IV malignant melanoma. The 100 mg concentrated injection is a new formulation being listed as a substitute for the currently listed 50 mg powder for injection.
icer_note: No economic analysis presented; minor submission for new formulation only
risk_sharing_note: Risk Sharing Arrangement currently in place for pembrolizumab for treatment of unresectable or metastatic melanoma; 100 mg formulation will be included in this arrangement with cost per patient capped under Deed of Agreement
pico_population: Adults with unresectable Stage III or Stage IV malignant melanoma; stratified by BRAF V600 mutation status, with requirement for prior BRAF inhibitor treatment (if BRAF-positive) and no prior ipilimumab or PD-1 inhibitor treatment

indication: Treatment of persistent, recurrent, or metastatic (Stage IVB) squamous cell carcinoma, adenocarcinoma and adenosquamous carcinoma of the cervix in patients whose tumours express PD-L1 combined positive score ≥1.
icer_note: ICER not stated in provided document excerpt
pico_population: Adults with persistent, recurrent or metastatic (Stage IVB) squamous cell carcinoma, adenocarcinoma or adenosquamous carcinoma of the cervix whose tumours express PD-L1 with combined positive score ≥1, unsuitable for curative treatment, with WHO performance status ≤1.

indication: Unresectable Stage III or Stage IV malignant melanoma. This is a minor submission requesting a change from weight-based dosing (2 mg/kg) to fixed 200 mg dosing.
icer_note: This is a minor submission on dosing regimen change; no new ICER calculated
risk_sharing_note: Managed Entry Scheme (MES) with risk sharing arrangement. Variation to deed took effect 1 April 2017 with adjusted annual expenditure caps.
pico_population: Adults with unresectable Stage III or Stage IV malignant melanoma, stratified by BRAF V600 mutation status. Initial treatment phases restricted to BRAF-mutant patients (post-BRAF inhibitor failure) or BRAF wild-type patients naive to ipilimumab or PD-1 inhibitors.

indication: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with inadequate glycaemic control as dual therapy in combination with metformin or a sulfonylurea (where either is contraindicated or not tolerated), or triple therapy in combination with metformin and a sulfonylurea.
